action.reasoning: The task requires producing a 4-chapter technical field guide with research, drafting, editing, a glossary, and assembly. This is too large for a single EXECUTE action. I will decompose it into parallel chapter subtasks (each handling research, draft, and edit for one chapter), a glossary subtask, and take the final assembly role myself. Each chapter subtask will produce its own chapter content. The glossary will be built in parallel. I will then assemble everything into the final guide.

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 1: Introduction to LLM Agent Memory Systems
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 1, which introduces the concept of memory in LLM agents, defines key terminology, and outlines the landscape of memory architectures. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 1 on introduction to LLM agent memory systems

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 2: Short-Term and Working Memory Mechanisms
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 2, covering short-term memory, context windows, attention mechanisms, and working memory patterns in LLM agents. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 2 on short-term and working memory mechanisms

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 3: Long-Term Memory Architectures
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 3, covering long-term memory systems including vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory stores, and retrieval-augmented generation patterns. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 3 on long-term memory architectures

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 4: Advanced Memory Patterns and Future Directions
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 4, covering advanced topics such as memory consolidation, multi-agent memory sharing, memory debugging, and emerging research directions. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 4 on advanced memory patterns and future directions

subtasks.goal: Create a unified glossary of terms used across all chapters
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces a comprehensive glossary of technical terms related to LLM agent memory systems. It should define terms consistently and be structured for easy reference. The glossary will be included in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Unified glossary of terms for the LLM agent memory field guide

self_role.goal: Assemble the final field guide from all chapter drafts and the glossary

outputs.description: The complete 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems, assembled from all chapter drafts and the glossary

action.reasoning: Chapter 3 is a substantial technical writing task covering vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory, and RAG patterns. It requires research, drafting, and editing. To manage scope and ensure quality, I will decompose into parallel research subtasks for each major topic, then have a dedicated drafting subtask and a final editing subtask. I will take the self_role of producing the chapter's internal outline and section summaries to guide the drafters.

subtasks.goal: Research vector database architectures for LLM long-term memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate vector databases (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) used for storing and retrieving semantic embeddings in agent memory systems. Cover indexing strategies (HNSW, IVF), similarity metrics, scalability, and trade-offs. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on vector database architectures for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research knowledge graph integration for agent memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate how knowledge graphs (e.g., Neo4j, RDF stores) are used to store structured, relational memory for agents. Cover schema design, graph traversal for retrieval, hybrid vector-graph approaches, and reasoning over graph structures. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on knowledge graph integration for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research episodic memory stores and RAG patterns
subtasks.capsule: Investigate episodic memory architectures (storing past interactions, experiences, and outcomes) and retrieval-augmented generation patterns for long-term memory. Cover memory consolidation, forgetting strategies, retrieval routing, and evaluation metrics. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on episodic memory stores and RAG patterns

subtasks.goal: Draft Chapter 3 based on research outputs
subtasks.capsule: Synthesize the three research summaries into a cohesive, well-structured draft of Chapter 3. Follow the outline provided by root.3/self_role. Ensure technical accuracy, clear explanations, and appropriate depth for a field guide audience. Include section headings, code examples where relevant, and cross-references.

outputs.description: Raw draft of Chapter 3 on long-term memory architectures

action.reasoning: Chapter 4 is a substantial piece of technical writing covering multiple advanced topics (consolidation, multi-agent sharing, debugging, future directions). To ensure depth and quality, I will decompose it into parallel research/drafting subtasks for each major topic, followed by an integration and editing pass. This allows parallel work on independent sections while ensuring a cohesive final product through a dedicated self-role for assembly and consistency editing.

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory consolidation and distillation techniques.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers how agents consolidate short-term memories into long-term structures, including summarization, hierarchical compression, and knowledge distillation methods. It should cover techniques like memory pruning, importance scoring, and periodic consolidation cycles. Output a well-researched draft section.

outputs.description: Draft section on memory consolidation and distillation techniques

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on multi-agent memory sharing and coordination.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers how multiple agents share, synchronize, and coordinate through shared memory systems. Topics include shared memory architectures, conflict resolution, memory ownership models, gossip protocols, and federated memory systems. Output a well-researched draft section.

outputs.description: Draft section on multi-agent memory sharing and coordination

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory debugging, observability, and evaluation.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers techniques for debugging agent memory systems, including memory tracing, observability tools, evaluation metrics for memory quality, common failure modes, and diagnostic patterns. Output a well-researched draft section.

outputs.description: Draft section on memory debugging, observability, and evaluation

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on emerging research directions and future trends.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers cutting-edge research directions in LLM agent memory, including neuro-symbolic memory, continuous learning without forgetting, memory-augmented architectures beyond RAG, and speculative future developments. Output a well-researched draft section.

outputs.description: Draft section on emerging research directions and future trends

self_role.goal: Integrate all section drafts into a cohesive Chapter 4, perform consistency editing, and produce the final chapter draft.
